mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals strong economic fundamentals and accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns show negative or neutral correlation with combined GDPC1/FEDFUNDS signals (Rank IC < 0.05), or individual series exhibit divergent directional impacts.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates tightening labor markets, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d returns show negative correlation with labor market strength signals (Rank IC < 0.03) or PAYEMS fails to predict SPX returns in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding corporate capacity utilization, which correlates with stronger earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO (Pearson r < -0.1) or industrial output fails to predict SPX returns in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals robust demand for goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d returns show negative correlation with combined HOUST/RSAFS signals (Rank IC < 0.05) or individual series exhibit divergent directional impacts.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly increase in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investor sentiment weakens.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns show positive correlation with UNRATE (Pearson r > 0.1) or unemployment fails to predict SPX returns in contractionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A 50bp increase in FEDFUNDS over 3 months signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS (Pearson r > 0.1) or rate hikes fail to predict SPX returns in tightening regimes.
